assignments.title: AnnieCannons New Student Information Quiz
assignments.description_html:
Welcome! Start-of-Term Expectations Quiz

We know you're receiving a lot of information at the beginning of the program. That’s normal, and we don’t expect you to memorize everything on day one. But we do want to make sure the most important details are easy to find, review, and remember.

This short quiz is here to help you pause and reflect on a few key expectations that shape how we work, communicate, and support each other in this program.

Everything in the quiz is covered in the slides, the handbook, or both.
You are encouraged to use your notes, refer back to the materials, and take your time.

You can take the quiz as many times as you need — the goal is to reach 100% so that we all start with a shared understanding.

assignments.title: Your Why
assignments.description_html:
This assignment is for the Career Development Manager's assignment, Your Why. 

Explore What Motivates You

Applying to AnnieCannons demonstrates your commitment to change and understanding your "why" can clarify your goals and impact, helping you stay focused and motivated through challenges.

Common Motivating Factors*:

  • It's the right time for a change.
  • I seek personal empowerment and confidence building.
  • I don't know what I want.
  • A major shift in my personal life is making me rethink my career.
  • I feel undervalued/underpaid.
  • I'm overworked and burned out.
  • I want more meaning and purpose.
  • I don't feel supported at work.
  • I'm not doing the work I want to do or am meant to do.
  • I want to shift roles and/or sectors.

*Note: It's common to experience more than one of these scenarios.

Activity: Prioritize your Motivations

Write about YOUR WHY and you may choose to list some factors that motivate you in your change right now! 

You completed the form here: https://forms.gle/HQJdA4GDST3rWp4K6 

What to turn in

There is nothing to turn in. When you submitted the form, you turned in the assignment. The CDM will confirm that you completed the work by marking this assignment complete.

assignments.title: Set up for success activity Copy
assignments.description_html:
In this assignment, you will apply time management and task prioritization strategies to enhance your productivity. You will also begin building your support team, a crucial step in your professional development. Follow the steps below and submit your work by the due date.

1. Time Blocking: Create Your Weekly Schedule

  • Objective: Develop a structured weekly schedule that includes time blocks for coding practice, job search activities, breaks, and personal time.
  • Instructions:
      1. Time Blocking: Create Your Weekly Schedule
        • Use the sample  weekly calendar template or the sample daily schedule to help guide you
        • Block out specific times for the following:
          • Coding practice
          • Job search activities
          • Breaks
          • Personal time
        • Objective: Develop a structured weekly schedule that includes time blocks for coding practice, job search activities, breaks, and personal time.
        • Instructions:
      Deliverable: Submit your completed weekly schedule as a PDF or URL link to Google Doc. below.

2. Task Prioritization: Create a Daily To-Do List using Notion

  • Objective: Develop a daily to-do list that prioritizes tasks based on urgency and importance.
  • Instructions:
    • Create a list of tasks for a typical day.
    • Prioritize these tasks to focus on high-impact activities first.
    • Deliverable: Submit your completed tasks as a Notion link below.

3. Build Your Team in Notion

  • Objective: Identify and document the members of your support team and their roles.
  • Instructions:
    • Use the page in your Notion Dashboard called "My Team" under the Career Development Section and list the people who are part of your support team, including their roles and how they will assist you.
    • Organize the list with Team names/roles on one side and How they might help/role on the other column 
    • Include any roles or support areas you are currently seeking to fill or developing-
    • Deliverable: Complete the team listing in your Notion instance and submit your shared Notion Team link below.

Optional: Pomodoro Method

  • Objective: Explore the Pomodoro Technique to enhance productivity.
  • Instructions:
    • Set up a Pomodoro timer using the tool available at Pomodoro Tracker.
    • Use this method to manage your time effectively for coding sprints or other focused tasks.
    • Deliverable: Optional. If you use the Pomodoro method, briefly describe your experience and any adjustments you made.

How to turn this in

In the text entry box (visible when you click "Start Assignment"), enter the following links, labeled with what each one goes to:

  • Your completed weekly schedule submit your completed weekly schedule as a PDF or URL link to google doc.
  • Your completed tasks TO DO List as a Notion link.
  • Your completed team listing Notion link.
  • Optional: If you use the Pomodoro method, briefly describe your experience and any adjustments you made underneath of your Notion links in the same text box.

assignments.title: Google Workspace: Drive
assignments.description_html:
This assignment is based on the "Google Workspace: Drive" course. Watch this course and complete the instructions below. 

 

1. Introduction to Google Workspace: Drive

  1. Log in to your Google account.
  2. Open Google Drive.
  3. Copy the starter projects.

2. Drive: Manage Files — Copy, Delete, Upload and Download Files

  1. Rename files.
  2. Make a copy of a file.
  3. Delete a file.
  4. Download a file to your computer.
  5. Upload a file from your computer.
  6. Convert the file to a Google Workspace file type.

3. Drive: Create and Manage Folders — Move, Rename, Delete and Upload Folders

  1. Create and name a folder for each file category and add files.
  2. Create and name a parent folder and move related folders into it.
  3. Remove a folder from your Drive.
  4. Upload a folder from your computer.

4. Drive: Change Display and Settings — Adjust Item View, Access Items Offline, and See Activity Updates

  1. Select grid or list view for your files and folders.
  2. View details about a file or folder.
  3. View recent activity for a file or folder.
  4. Make Drive available offline. (Use this link: https://chromewebstore.google.com/detail/google-docs-offline/ghbmnnjooekpmoecnnnilnnbdlolhkhi)
  5. Turn on notifications.

assignments.title: Week 3 Blogging - AI
assignments.description_html:
Use your blog inside of Notion to think about the questions below. Write a blog post addressing these questions in any way that makes sense to you. You are not required to answer all questions, just the ones that you feel are relevant.

This week you explored AI capabilities, applications, and limitations. Consider your experience learning about and using AI tools during this course. Reflect on how this knowledge shapes your perspective on AI, and your approach to using it effectively while maintaining a healthy skepticism about its accuracy.

Consider these questions as you write:

  • What surprised you most about AI's capabilities? Share a specific example that made you think "wow, that's incredible!"
  • How do you plan to use AI tools responsibly in your academic work or future career? 
  • Describe a time during the course when you discovered AI made an error or gave questionable advice. How did this change your perspective on trusting AI outputs?
  • If you were explaining AI to a friend who's never used it, what would be your top 3 pieces of advice?

If this does not resonate

If you are having a hard time getting started with this blogging prompt, see if you can write an explanation why. Then try answering one of these alternative prompts:

  • What was your biggest win this week? 
  • What did you struggle with this week?
  • What was an interesting article you read or a video you watched this week?
  • What do you wish you had more time to work on? Include links/screenshots of the item and a description of what you’d like to add/change/improve if you had more time.
  • Describe one of the concepts you learned this week in your own words.
  • Read your past blog entries and reflect on where you are now in the learning process.

How to turn this in

Copy a direct URL for your Notion blog page and submit it below. Be sure that your instructor(s) and TA (if applicable) are able to access your post!

assignments.description_html:
In this assignment, you will apply time management and task prioritization strategies to enhance your productivity. You will also begin building your support team, a crucial step in your professional development. Follow the steps below and submit your work by the due date.

1. Time Blocking: Create Your Weekly Schedule

  • Objective: Develop a structured weekly schedule that includes time blocks for coding practice, job search activities, breaks, and personal time.
  • Instructions:
      1. Time Blocking: Create Your Weekly Schedule
        • Use the sample  weekly calendar template or the sample daily schedule to help guide you
        • Block out specific times for the following:
          • Coding practice
          • Job search activities
          • Breaks
          • Personal time
        • Objective: Develop a structured weekly schedule that includes time blocks for coding practice, job search activities, breaks, and personal time.
        • Instructions:
      Deliverable: Submit your completed weekly schedule as a PDF or URL link to Google Doc. below.

2. Task Prioritization: Create a Daily To-Do List using Notion

  • Objective: Develop a daily to-do list that prioritizes tasks based on urgency and importance.
  • Instructions:
    • Create a list of tasks for a typical day.
    • Prioritize these tasks to focus on high-impact activities first.
    • Deliverable: Submit your completed tasks as a Notion link below.

3. Build Your Team in Notion

  • Objective: Identify and document the members of your support team and their roles.
  • Instructions:
    • Use the page in your Notion Dashboard called "My Team" under the Career Development Section and list the people who are part of your support team, including their roles and how they will assist you.
    • Organize the list with Team names/roles on one side and How they might help/role on the other column 
    • Include any roles or support areas you are currently seeking to fill or developing-
    • Deliverable: Complete the team listing in your Notion instance and submit your shared Notion Team link below.

Optional: Pomodoro Method

  • Objective: Explore the Pomodoro Technique to enhance productivity.
  • Instructions:
    • Set up a Pomodoro timer using the tool available at Pomodoro Tracker.
    • Use this method to manage your time effectively for coding sprints or other focused tasks.
    • Deliverable: Optional. If you use the Pomodoro method, briefly describe your experience and any adjustments you made.

How to turn this in

In the text entry box (visible when you click "Start Assignment"), enter the following links, labeled with what each one goes to:

assignments.title: CodePen: Pumpkin Pie Debugging Exercise
assignments.description_html:
Fork and save this pen to your account: https://codepen.io/team/anniecannons/pen/VwOoYPo

The HTML in this CodePen contains many errors.

Your job is to debug the HTML and fix the errors.

Suggested approach:

  • Look at the HTML and fix all of the errors you see immediately.
  • Use "Analyze HTML" in CodePen to help you identify errors you've missed.
  • Rather than trying to fix every error at once, fix one error, then re-run Analyze HTML. You will find that some "errors" are not truly there. This is because a previous error made the software think another error was present.

In general, look for these kinds of errors:

  • Unclosed tags
  • Poor syntax (the punctuation that makes up HTML)
  • Bad nesting of HTML elements (remember that the tags close in the opposite order in which they are opened)
  • The wrong element used for the situation

When you are finished, turn in the FORKED AND SAVED CodePen below.

assignments.title: Future Me Letter
assignments.description_html:
Write a reflective letter to future you that you will read at the end of this course. This letter should help you set goals, outline your expectations, and encourage yourself through any challenges.

    1. Open your AnnieCannons Gmail
    2. Click “Compose” to write a new email
    3. In the “To” field, type in your own business email
    4. In the “Subject” field, type “ITP Future Me Letter”
    5. Write your letter
    6. To schedule send, click the down arrow next to blue send button
    7. Click “Schedule send”
    8. Click “Pick date & time”
    9. Select “April 23, 2026” as the date
    10. Select “9:00 AM” as the time
    11. Click the blue "Schedule Send" button

 

How to turn this in

    5. Take a screenshot of the details box and submit. You do not need to show your letter, only the details box showing time, date, recipient, and subject line
